likely due to deconditioning and difficulty following instructions on exam.

tab; donepezil 5 mg; escitalopram 5 mg; trazodone as needed qhs.

declines placement in assisted/memory care due to cost.
son declines losing P3HP as PCP, declining hospice care but willing to consider Palliative medicine only.

agreeing to sign papers for guardianship.
in the meantime, son has verbalized patient to have DNR status. POLST done today reflects the same.

denies LOC, pain in back or hips or arm.

patient is actually functionally improved with PT--now able to move, and stand and more mobile. knee joints ROM have

straighter her legs comfortably and able to sit cross-legged.
seen by GI Dr. Chen and was recommended colonography. when son made appt with radiology, per son, radiology

the pessary is becoming more difficult to remove and they are recommended catheterization instead. patient's son is
asking for clarification and maybe a second opinion.
unfortunatley, son wishes to continue with pessary and was referred to a Urologist. he is not satisfied with care by Dr.
